guidances.note: Board approved evaluating divestment/sale/transfer/judicial reorganization options for French subsidiary Centum T&S; no firm timeline given.
Management: 'Based on our present assessment, we do not expect any further material financial impact beyond what has already been recognized.'

France entered Redressement Judiciaire (court-supervised process) in March 2026; final bids due to the Lyon court by 1 June 2026; 'we expect the divestment process to be substantially concluded by July 2026.' Court-approved plan (10 June 2026) has MBDA France and SII acquiring the main activities.

Contrary to the Q3 FY26 assessment, additional impairments/operating losses were recognized in Q4 FY26 as the French subsidiary was reclassified to discontinued operations (goodwill/intangible impairment, inventory write-off, ~Rs1,525mn 'discontinuing operations' impact disclosed in the Q4 investor presentation).
Canada operations discontinued in Q4 FY26; wind-up proceedings to commence in Q1 FY27.

Management: 'we're targeting to be in the range of 13% to 15% at a combined level and we are slightly below that for the full year' -- FY26 standalone EBITDA margin came in at 12.42%, below the guided range, due to EMS product mix.

Management reiterated the same 13-15% EBITDA margin target for FY27: 'that's what we're working towards, and we maintain that target' -- and again for the next 2-3 years in response to a later question.
Standing medium-term standalone growth target of 25-30% CAGR, reiterated at both Q3 and Q4 FY26 calls; FY26 actual standalone revenue growth of +25.4% YoY sits at the low end of this range.
Declared L1 bidder for development & production of a complete radar system for a major helicopter/airborne platform of a defense PSU; total program value estimated at ~Rs700cr over 5-6 years.

L1 bid converted into a confirmed order: HAL AESA Radar for the UHM platform, total opportunity Rs570+cr -- development phase (~Rs66-67cr) over 2 years, production phase (~Rs500cr+) executed through FY30/31. Full program still mostly ahead; only the order win itself is delivered.

GRSE air navigation program: first order ~Rs30cr received; total opportunity ~Rs500cr over 3-5 years across shipyards (GRSE, MDL, GSL, HSL). 'We expect to receive orders from several other shipyards including GSL and HSL in coming quarters.'
Space-Based Surveillance (SBS) opportunity sized at ~Rs1,000cr; 'We expect orders to start coming in Q4 and subsequent quarters.'
Secured second complete radar system order for satellite and space debris tracking (~Rs30cr) -- a first tranche of the Rs1,000cr SBS opportunity converting to firm orders as expected.
Analyst asked about ~USD30mn annual revenue target in 2-3 years from anchor semiconductor equipment customer.

Management: 'we are progressing quite well, I would say, broadly in line with our expectations for the growth from this customer segment' -- discussions begun with a couple of other semiconductor customers, still preliminary.
